mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, short rate expectations ease and equity discount rates decline, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because disinflation reduces the chance of renewed tightening.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces near-term earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the sign of the effect flips in a 2-year rolling window without a change in unemployment trend.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates stronger payroll income and retail demand, which supports revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-plus-RSAFS signal fails to outperform each single-series proxy alone, or if 5-day forward returns are not positive in the top signal quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO with stable inflation indicates improving real output without immediate policy pressure, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through better earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if periods of rising INDPRO and flat CPIAUCSL do not deliver above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when inflation is unchanged.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST drops sharply, it signals weakening housing demand and broader cyclical deceleration, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by at least 8% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if sharp HOUST declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than half of triggered cases, or if no negative skew appears versus the unconditional sample.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate combined with rising CPIAUCSL creates a tightening trap: real policy pressure stays restrictive and equity multiples contract, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is rising on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate high-inflation episodes do not underperform within 60 trading days, or if the interaction term is not materially worse than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS over the most recent month signals weakening payroll growth and reduced earnings momentum, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the signal has no edge over three years of walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong concurrent readings in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicate resilient aggregate demand, and that demand resilience should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days by preserving revenue growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-and-sales signal fails to outperform unconditional 60-day returns, or if strong-demand episodes do not show positive return skew across rolling out-of-sample windows.
